paper_map.title: Extreme high accuracy prediction and design of Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si steel using machine learning
paper_map.abstract_summary: This study develops a data-driven model combining machine learning (ML), firefly optimization algorithm (FA), and conditional generative adversarial networks (CGANs) to predict solid solution strengthening theory for Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si steel. Support vector regression (SVR) achieved the highest microhardness prediction accuracy, with R² improving from 0.85 to 0.89 and RMSE decreasing from 0.39 to 0.31 after introducing a modified solid solution strengthening theory. Three alloys were fabricated by cladding for experimental validation, yielding a minimum prediction error of 1.17%. The approach accelerates the design of Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si steel with extreme high accuracy.
paper_map.research_objective: To develop an alternate approach combining machine learning, firefly optimization, and CGANs to rapidly and accurately predict the solid solution strengthening theory and microhardness of Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si steel, overcoming the limitations of time-consuming and costly experimental determination.
main_process_variables: Chemical composition (C, Si, Mn, Cr, S, Ni, P, Fe, Mo, V, W mass percentages)
Modified solid solution strengthening theory (difference in cohesive energy, local cohesive energy mismatch, shear modulus)
sample_aliases: Sample 1: Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si alloy with no Ni, no Mo, no V, no W
Sample 2: Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si alloy with Ni, V, W
Sample 3: Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si alloy with Ni, Mo, V
expected_information_axis: mechanism (solid solution strengthening, cohesive energy, shear modulus)
key_sections: 1. Introduction: literature review on ML for steel prediction and solid solution strengthening theory, research gap

2. Materials and methodology: 2.1 Construction of the ML model (2.1.1 Data collection and pre-processing; 2.1.2 Feature engineering; 2.1.3 Algorithm selection and optimization); 2.2 Inverse design; 2.3 Coating preparation and characterization

3. Results and discussion: 3.1 ML algorithm modeling (3.1.1 Screening results of elements; 3.1.2 Screening results of features; 3.1.3 Screening results of models; 3.1.4 Model optimization by solid solution strengthening theory; 3.1.5 Screening results of inverse design); 3.2 Experimental verification
4. Conclusions: summary of key findings (Cr impact, modified theory, prediction accuracy, experimental validation)
